content.problemStatement:
For an undirected tree with NNN nodes and N−1N-1N−1 edges, where each node is colored either red or blue, define two sets S1S_1S1​ and S2S_2S2​ as follows:


S1={dis(u,v)∣1≤u<v≤N}S_1 = \{dis(u,v) \mid 1 \leq u \lt v \leq N\}S1​={dis(u,v)∣1≤u<v≤N}
S2={dis(u,v)∣1≤u<v≤N,coloru≠colorv}S_2 = \{dis(u,v) \mid 1 \leq u \lt v \leq N, color_u \neq color_v\}S2​={dis(u,v)∣1≤u<v≤N,coloru​=colorv​} ,

where dis(u,v)=dis(u,v) = dis(u,v)= number of edges on the simple path between uuu and vvv.
**Note:** Sets S1S_1S1​ and S2S_2S2​ only contain **distinct** elements.

We call the coloring good if S1=S2S_1 = S_2S1​=S2​.

Let CRC_RCR​ and CBC_BCB​ denote the number of nodes that are colored red and blue respectively.

Given an undirected tree with NNN nodes and N−1N-1N−1 edges, find a good coloring of the tree such that the value of ∣CR−CB∣|C_R - C_B|∣CR​−CB​∣ is **minimized**.

It can be shown that a good coloring always exists. If there are multiple good colorings with the minimum value of ∣CR−CB∣|C_R - C_B|∣CR​−CB​∣, you may output any of them.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the number of vertices of the tree.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines describe the edges of the tree. The ithi^{th}ith of these N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between nodes uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output a string SSS of length NNN, where Si=RS_i = RSi​=R if node iii is colored red or Si=BS_i = BSi​=B if node iii is colored blue.


Note that string SSS should represent a good coloring of the tree such that the value of ∣CR−CB∣|C_R - C_B|∣CR​−CB​∣ is **minimized**.


If there are multiple good colorings with the minimum value of ∣CR−CB∣|C_R - C_B|∣CR​−CB​∣, you may output any possible string.
constraints: The edges in the input form a tree.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 200020002000.
